story.title: Dr. Chen and the Hot Car Puzzle
story.text:
Hello! I’m Dr. Sarah Chen, a scientist at the National Science Lab. My job is to understand how different parts work together to make something happen. It’s like being a detective for science!

Today, let’s solve a sunny day puzzle. Have you ever touched a black car sitting in the sun? Ouch! It can be burning hot. But a white car right next to it feels much cooler. Why is that?

Let’s look at the system step-by-step.
1. First, think about the sun. It sends powerful light energy down to Earth.
2. Next, this energy hits the two cars. The car’s surface color decides what happens next.
3. The black car’s dark surface is great at heat absorption. This means it grabs and holds onto most of the light energy that hits it. It soaks it up like a sponge.

What does a dark surface do with sunlight?

4. That stored light energy changes into thermal energy, which we feel as heat. More absorption means the surface temperature gets much higher.
5. Now, look at the white car. Its light surface does the opposite. It is not good at absorbing heat. Instead, it is good at reflection. Most of the light energy bounces right off!

Because the black car absorbs more energy, it gets hotter. The white car reflects more energy, so it stays cooler. Understanding this helps us build cooler cities and homes!
think_abouts: Why is it a good idea to wear a white t-shirt on a hot, sunny day?
What other dark-colored things have you noticed get very hot in the sun?
story.go_deeper: How could engineers use this idea about color to help keep buildings cooler in the summer?

margin_defs.definition: to take in or soak up

check_prompts: What does a dark surface do with sunlight?

vocabulary.definition: Energy from the sun that travels as light and can turn into heat.
vocabulary.use_sentence: The sun sends light energy to the Earth every day.

vocabulary.definition: When an object takes in light energy and turns it into heat.
vocabulary.use_sentence: The black pavement has high heat absorption on a sunny day.

vocabulary.definition: How light or dark the outside of an object is.
vocabulary.use_sentence: The surface color of the snow was bright white.

vocabulary.definition: Energy that we feel as heat.
vocabulary.use_sentence: A cup of hot chocolate has a lot of thermal energy.

vocabulary.definition: When light bounces off an object instead of being taken in.
vocabulary.use_sentence: I could see the reflection of the sun on the water.

discussion_cards.prompt: If you were designing a new playground for a very sunny park, what colors would you choose for the slide? Why?
discussion_cards.frame: I would choose ___ colors for the slide because ___.

discussion_cards.prompt: Dr. Chen studies how parts of a system work together. How do sunlight and surface color work together to change the temperature of a car?
discussion_cards.frame: Sunlight and surface color work together when the sun's energy hits the car, and then the color ___.

reflection_frames.knew: Before reading, I already knew that ___
reflection_frames.learned: From the story, I learned that ___
